Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a config/API replacement PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main still uses the old `documents` surface that the PR replaces.

target.title: Replace documents with workspace bootstrap files
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the removed `programs.openclaw.documents` directory API with explicit `programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles`.
- Adds `programs.openclaw.workspace.files` for extra Nix-managed workspace files that are not upstream bootstrap files.
- Forces `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true` when Nix manages bootstrap files, and fails eval if user config tries to set it false.
- Updates the agent-first template, README, golden-path docs, and focused module/activation checks.

## Breaking API

`programs.openclaw.documents` now fails with a migration message. Required managed bootstrap files are explicit: `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md`. `HEARTBEAT.md` is optional and managed only when `workspace.bootstrapFiles.heartbeat` is set.

`BOOTSTRAP.md` and `MEMORY.md` are left runtime-owned and cannot be managed through `workspace.files`.

## Review Notes

- Refreshed `/Users/josh/code/openclaw` with `git pull --ff-only`; it was already up to date.
- Checked upstream docs/code vocabulary: current OpenClaw uses “workspace bootstrap files” and documents `skipBootstrap`.
- Reviewed old `documents` history with `git log -S documents`; this replaces the older directory-inference surface rather than preserving it.
- Ran three adversarial passes:
  - API/lifecycle review against upstream workspace bootstrap behavior.
  - Nix/materializer edge-case review; fixed invalid `workspace.files` keys such as `""`, `"."`, `../`, trailing slash, tabs, and newlines before PR.
  - ClawSweeper-style merge-risk preflight with current local ClawSweeper prompts/policies.

## Tests

- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.workspace-materializer --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- `nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.hm-activation --accept-flake-config --no-link --dry-run`

## Human Written Summary

The intent of this change is, as written by a human:

> If skipBootstrap = false, OpenClaw may create missing bootstrap files from bundled templates.  -> i think we should NEVER let openclaw do that because it fucks up the declarative/nix ownership for the future
>
> The tradeoff: it disables OpenClaw’s first-run BOOTSTRAP.md ritual for Nix-managed installs. I think that is correct for the declarative path. The template should create starter files in the repo instead. -> this is fine, the bootstrap ritual is known not working
>
> programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles = {
>   agents = ./workspace/AGENTS.md;
>   soul = ./workspace/SOUL.md;
>   tools = ./workspace/TOOLS.md;
>   identity = ./workspace/IDENTITY.md;
>   user = ./workspace/USER.md;
>
>   heartbeat = null;
> };
>
> programs.openclaw.workspace.files = {
>   "LORE.md" = ./workspace/LORE.md;
>   "PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md" = ./workspace/PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md;
> };
>
> -> explicit is better. zen of python. explicit over implicit.
>
> they have coding agents they can use them.

## AI Disclosure

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
